title: Address sanitizer upset on bit-select operations over-reading memory
body:
I found some strange behavior when running a project, which made me suspect there was buffer overflow bugs. Using address sanitizer, I find the following code can let ASAN report a buffer overflow. Tested on verilator 4.108 and git revision v4.110-14-g018d9947.

top.sv:
```
`define uWidth 35
`define vLen 16
`define vecBits (`uWidth*`vLen)

class MyClass;
	task run(input bit [`vecBits-1:0] iReg, input int num);
		int i,knum;
		bit [`uWidth-1:0] t;

		knum = (num>`vLen)?`vLen:num;
		for (i = 0; i < knum; i++) begin
			$display("%d",i);
			t = iReg[`uWidth*i+:`uWidth];
			$display("%x",t);
		end
	endtask
endclass;

module top;
	bit [559:0] v;
	int i;
initial begin
	MyClass o;
	o = new;
	v = 560'b0;
	/* it's strage that using the following will not trigger an overflow
	for (i = 0; i < `vLen; i++) begin
		v[`uWidth*i+:`uWidth] = {i[2:0],32'h0};
	end
	*/
	o.run(v,16);
	$finish();
end
endmodule;
```

main.cc:
```
#include "Vtop.h"      // From Verilating "top.v"
#include <verilated.h> // Defines common routines

Vtop *top; // Instantiation of module

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
  Verilated::commandArgs(argc, argv); // Remember args

  top = new Vtop; // Create instance

  while (!Verilated::gotFinish()) {
    top->eval();              // Evaluate model
  }

  top->final(); // Done simulating
  //    // (Though this example doesn't get here)
  delete top;
}
```

sanitizer report:
```
$ verilator --cc --exe top.sv main.cc 
$ make -C obj_dir/ -f Vtop.mk CXXFLAGS='-fsanitize=address' USER_LDFLAGS='-fsanitize=address' OPT_FAST='-O0 -g' 
$ ./obj_dir/Vtop 
          0
000000000
          1
000000000
          2
000000000
          3
000000000
          4
000000000
          5
000000000
          6
000000000
          7
000000000
          8
000000000
          9
000000000
         10
000000000
         11
000000000
         12
000000000
         13
000000000
         14
000000000
         15
=================================================================
==4609==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: stack-buffer-overflow on address 0x7ffe59c382b8 at pc 0x55cb26a17f73 bp 0x7ffe59c38120 sp 0x7ffe59c38110
READ of size 4 at 0x7ffe59c382b8 thread T0
    #0 0x55cb26a17f72 in Vtop___024unit__03a__03aMyClass::__VnoInFunc_run(Vtop__Syms*, unsigned int*, unsigned int) /home/irudog/programs/verilog/sv/test_overflow/obj_dir/Vtop___024unit__03a__03aMyClass__Vclpkg.cpp:34
    #1 0x55cb26a191a5 in Vtop::_initial__TOP__1(Vtop__Syms*) /home/irudog/programs/verilog/sv/test_overflow/obj_dir/Vtop__Slow.cpp:60
    #2 0x55cb26a19356 in Vtop::_eval_initial(Vtop__Syms*) /home/irudog/programs/verilog/sv/test_overflow/obj_dir/Vtop__Slow.cpp:68
    #3 0x55cb26a17c52 in Vtop::_eval_initial_loop(Vtop__Syms*) /home/irudog/programs/verilog/sv/test_overflow/obj_dir/Vtop.cpp:46
    #4 0x55cb26a17b57 in Vtop::eval_step() /home/irudog/programs/verilog/sv/test_overflow/obj_dir/Vtop.cpp:21
    #5 0x55cb269fbe87 in Vtop::eval() Vtop.h:52
    #6 0x55cb269fbd69 in main ../main.cc:12
    #7 0x7fafebfedb24 in __libc_start_main (/usr/lib/libc.so.6+0x27b24)
    #8 0x55cb269fbafd in _start (/home/irudog/programs/verilog/sv/test_overflow/obj_dir/Vtop+0x8afd)

Address 0x7ffe59c382b8 is located in stack of thread T0 at offset 248 in frame
    #0 0x55cb26a18ab5 in Vtop::_initial__TOP__1(Vtop__Syms*) /home/irudog/programs/verilog/sv/test_overflow/obj_dir/Vtop__Slow.cpp:35

  This frame has 5 object(s):
    [48, 56) 'vlSymsp' (line 35)
    [80, 96) '<unknown>'
    [112, 128) '<unknown>'
    [144, 160) '<unknown>'
    [176, 248) '__Vtemp1' (line 39) <== Memory access at offset 248 overflows this variable
HINT: this may be a false positive if your program uses some custom stack unwind mechanism, swapcontext or vfork
      (longjmp and C++ exceptions *are* supported)
SUMMARY: AddressSanitizer: stack-buffer-overflow /home/irudog/programs/verilog/sv/test_overflow/obj_dir/Vtop___024unit__03a__03aMyClass__Vclpkg.cpp:34 in Vtop___024unit__03a__03aMyClass::__VnoInFunc_run(Vtop__Syms*, unsigned int*, unsigned int)
Shadow bytes around the buggy address:
  0x10004b37f000: 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
  0x10004b37f010: 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
  0x10004b37f020: 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
  0x10004b37f030: 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 f1 f1 f1 f1 00 00 00 f2
  0x10004b37f040: f2 f2 f8 f8 f2 f2 00 00 f2 f2 00 00 f2 f2 00 00
=>0x10004b37f050: 00 00 00 00 00 00 00[f3]f3 f3 f3 f3 00 00 00 00
  0x10004b37f060: 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
  0x10004b37f070: 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
  0x10004b37f080: 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
  0x10004b37f090: 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
  0x10004b37f0a0: 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
Shadow byte legend (one shadow byte represents 8 application bytes):
  Addressable:           00
  Partially addressable: 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 
  Heap left redzone:       fa
  Freed heap region:       fd
  Stack left redzone:      f1
  Stack mid redzone:       f2
  Stack right redzone:     f3
  Stack after return:      f5
  Stack use after scope:   f8
  Global redzone:          f9
  Global init order:       f6
  Poisoned by user:        f7
  Container overflow:      fc
  Array cookie:            ac
  Intra object redzone:    bb
  ASan internal:           fe
  Left alloca redzone:     ca
  Right alloca redzone:    cb
  Shadow gap:              cc
==4609==ABORTING
```

The reason for the buffer overflow is that the generated code has ``iReg[((IData)(2U) + (0x1fU & (VL_MULS_III(10,32,32, (IData)(0x23U), i) >> 5U)))]`` where ``iReg`` is of size 18, and when ``i`` is 15, it'll access ``iReg[18]``, which is an overflow.
